preamble: The transformation from a voiceless uvular plosive [q] to a voiceless uvular fricative [χ] is a well-attested phonetic shift, primarily driven by lenition. This sound change involves a reduction in the degree of obstruction in the vocal tract, moving from a complete closure (plosive) to a partial constriction allowing turbulent airflow (fricative).

examples.note: Shift of /q/ to /χ/ in all positions.
Shift of /q/ to /χ/ in all positions.

sources: Kloekhorst, A. (2018). Anatolian evidence suggests that the Indo-European laryngeals *h2 and *h3 were uvular stops. Indo-European Linguistics, 6, 69–94.
